replication_study.hypothesis: Among U.S. adults completing two web-based surveys one-week apart, higher perceived COVID-19 realistic threat at Time 1 will predict higher total negative affect (PANAS negative items) one week later at Time 2.

data_plan.source_type: Online self-report questionnaire administered through Prolific Academic
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Wave 1 (baseline) and Wave 2 (≈7-day follow-up) contained in the file; only participants with two completed waves will be analysed.
data_plan.sample_size: 633 rows total; ≈240 unique participants with 2 waves after cleaning (exact number printed by script).

qualification.explanation: The dataset contains the same survey instrument (identical item names) collected from a fresh Prolific panel about nine months after the original MTurk sample, providing an independent but comparable sample for replication.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Item names (covid_real1-5, negative1-10) and scoring rules match those described for the original study (post_registration.json, Materials section of original_paper.pdf).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data (post_registration.json) were collected March 2020 on MTurk; replication data were collected January 2021 on Prolific, include different participant pool and later pandemic phase, plus slightly smaller longitudinal sample.

data_plan.notes: Dataset already de-identified; date column requires POSIX parsing. Some rows represent pilot/testing sessions that will be dropped by time-stamp filter inside script.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load CSV; 2) Compute Realistic threat (mean of covid_real1-5) and Negative affect (sum of negative1-10) per response; 3) Order responses by created timestamp within participant to label Wave 1 and Wave 2; 4) Keep participants with two waves; 5) Build wide file with Realistic_T1 and Negative_T2; 6) Estimate OLS regression Negative_T2 ~ Realistic_T1; 7) Record coefficient, SE, t, p.
planned_method.models: Ordinary least squares regression (optionally add Symbolic_T1 as covariate robustness check).
planned_method.outcome_variable: Negative affect at Time 2 (sum of PANAS negative items 1–10).
planned_method.independent_variables: Realistic threat at Time 1 (mean of covid_real1-5).
planned_method.control_variables: None in focal test (Symbolic threat may be included as robustness).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Unstandardised OLS slope coefficient for Realistic_T1 predicting Negative_T2.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: List-wise deletion for rows missing Realistic_T1 or Negative_T2; composite scores use mean/sum with skip-na.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicated if coefficient is positive and p < .05; effect size compared to original b = 4.02.

files.replication_data/replication_analysis.py: End-to-end Python script that scores variables, reshapes data to wide form, runs OLS Negative_T2 ~ Realistic_T1, prints and saves summary to /app/data/replication_results.txt.

codebase.notes: Script relies only on pandas, numpy, statsmodels; all file I/O confined to /app/data path.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare sign and statistical significance of Realistic threat coefficient; report difference between replication b̂ and original b = 4.02 as absolute and percentage change.
